topic.what: Authenticating non-deterministic computations via non-deterministic lattice encodings supporting randomized function evaluation
topic.how: Extends homomorphic lattice encodings using decomposed LWE hardness; constructs laconic shift-hiding PRFs, compressed LWE sampling, adaptively-secure broadcast encryption, and pseudorandom obfuscation for puncturable PRFs
topic.why_matters: Enables cryptographic primitives previously unknown from lattice assumptions; first lattice-based adaptively-secure broadcast encryption in plain model; progress on obfuscation constructions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Broadcast encryption ciphertext size relative to number of users
characteristics.vs_baseline: First lattice-based construction achieving this bound

characteristics.what_it_measures: Public/private key size in broadcast encryption

characteristics.what_itasures: Depth complexity for path resolution in non-deterministic encodings
characteristics.vs_baseline: Circuit commitments achieved log d · poly(log delta)

characteristics.context: Compress-and-expand LWE samples in plain model

novelty: Non-deterministic encodings for authenticating randomized computations
First laconic shift-hiding PRF from lattice assumptions
First lattice-based adaptively-secure broadcast encryption in plain model
Pseudorandom obfuscation for all puncturable PRFs from standard assumptions
Heuristic direct lattice-based iO construction bypassing FE-to-iO paradigm
open_problems: Reduce CRS size to poly-logarithmic in N for adaptively-secure broadcast encryption
Prove security of heuristic iO construction
Extend pseudorandom obfuscation beyond puncturable functions
Full security reduction for general obfuscation
